name: Trujillo – city tour circular by the historic quarter
name_original: Trujillo – city tour circular por el casco histórico
region_intro: Hiking around Trujillo, Spain, offers routes through a historic hill town set amidst rocky terrain and wide plains. The region is characterized by its prominent hill, providing commanding viewpoints over the Extremaduran landscape. Trails often integrate the town's historic center, including the Plaza Mayor and ancient fortifications, with surrounding natural features like the Berrocal. This blend provides diverse hiking experiences, from urban exploration to paths through the local countryside. Best hiking trails around Trujillo The most popular hiking route is Trujillo – city tour circular by the historic quarter, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the town's ancient streets and landmarks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Plaza Mayor of Trujillo – San Andrés Gate loop from Trujillo, an easy 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This route guides hikers through the historic Plaza Mayor and past the San Andrés Gate, offering views of the town's heritage. Local hikers also love the San Andrés Gate loop from Trujillo, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through the historic town center, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Hiking around Trujillo is defined by its historic hill town setting, rocky terrain, and views over wide plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban walks to moderate trails exploring the surrounding landscape. The routes in Trujillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from nearly 20 reviews. More than 160 hikers have used komoot to explore Trujillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Trujillo?
A: Hiking around Trujillo, Spain, offers a blend of historic urban landscapes and natural rocky terrain. You'll find routes through the ancient hill town, providing commanding viewpoints over the extensive Extremaduran plains. Many trails integrate the town's historic center, including the Plaza Mayor, with surrounding natural features like the 'berrocal' – a rocky area characteristic of the region.

Q: How many hiking trails are available around Trujillo?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails available around Trujillo, offering diverse experiences from easy urban walks to moderate trails exploring the surrounding landscape. The komoot community has explored 23 different routes in the area.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Trujillo?
A: Yes, Trujillo offers several easy routes suitable for families. Many trails explore the historic town center, which can be a great way to engage children with the local history and architecture. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990873925/trujillo-city-tour-circular-by-the-historic-quarter" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo – city tour circular by the historic quarter&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-mile route that takes you through ancient streets and landmarks.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Trujillo?
A: Absolutely. Many of the hiking routes in Trujillo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428922" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Trujillo – San Andrés Gate loop from Trujillo&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.4-mile path guiding you through the historic Plaza Mayor and past the San Andrés Gate.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in Trujillo?
A: Trujillo's hiking routes often weave through its rich history. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3254281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trujillo Main Square&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4461605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6470400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Andrés Gate&lt;/a&gt;. The town's fortified castle, built on Roman remains, also offers stunning views of the Extremaduran landscape and is often integrated into walking routes.

Q: Are there any easy or accessible routes for beginners in Trujillo?
A: Yes, Trujillo has a good selection of easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. Out of 23 total tours, 13 are rated as easy. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Convent – Plaza Mayor of Trujillo loop from Trujillo&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter, easy route exploring the town's core.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Trujillo?
A: The hiking routes in Trujillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from nearly 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique blend of historical exploration and scenic views over the Extremaduran landscape, particularly enjoying the well-integrated urban and natural paths.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Trujillo?
A: The best time to hike in Trujillo is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, allowing you to comfortably explore both the historic town and the surrounding natural areas. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and sometimes wet.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Trujillo?
A: As Trujillo is a historic town, parking can be found in and around the town center, which often serves as the starting point for many hiking routes. It's advisable to look for public parking areas or designated spots within walking distance of the main attractions and trail access points.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Trujillo?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Spain, especially in natural areas, provided they are kept under control. For urban routes within Trujillo, it's best to keep dogs on a leash. Always ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the cleanliness of the trails and town.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the hiking routes in Trujillo?
A: Many of Trujillo's hiking routes pass directly through or near the historic town center, including the Plaza Mayor. This means you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and bars where you can stop for refreshments, a meal, or a coffee break during or after your hike. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38224875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alberca – Plaza Mayor of Trujillo loop from Trujillo&lt;/a&gt; offer easy access to these amenities.
tips: The Church of the Incarnation of Trujillo was founded as the Convent of the Incarnation of Dominican friars in the 18th century, later, it was looted and destroyed by the French in 1809 when the War of Independence broke out. Also the English army used it as a barracks in 1836.

Possibly built on one of the Arab mosques that existed in Trujillo. It was built in the 13th century, after the conquest of the city. The church retains Romanesque and Gothic elements, but underwent major restoration in the 16th century. It has three naves with three sections and a polygonal apse, covered with ribbed vaults with tiercerons. The rose window stands out on the cover. Inside, the main Gothic altarpiece stands out, executed in the workshop of Fernando Gállego and the Plateresque choir.

Erected on the upper level of the Trujillo ridge, on a hill called "Cabeza de Zorro", the Alcazaba de Trujillo is an Andalusian monument from the Umayyad period, erected sometime between the ninth and tenth centuries.

The foundation of this house of nuns of the Third Franciscan Order, under the invocation of San Pedro, dates back to the final years of the 15th century, in the time of Isabel I of Castile. Some documents cite the parish priest of Santa María La Mayor as their first benefactor

In the monumental city of Trujillo, cradle of discoverers, you will find a beautiful Parador over the 16th century convent of Santa Clara, preserving the atmosphere of peace and tranquility that its conventual structure gives it. Inside, you will be surprised by two beautiful cloisters, one of them with Renaissance arches and columns; the second provides the whole with a note of luminosity that embellishes the environment.

The Palacio de los Barrantes-Cervantes was created between 1615 and 1618 by García Carrasco, who was in charge of its fantastic façades.
The palace was rehabilitated by the Obra Pía Foundation and today it is used as the historical headquarters of the foundation, which has been related to Latin America and Trujillo since the 16th century.

Craft center located in what was the church of the Convent of La Merced, dedicated to the exhibition and sale. The convent was founded in the 17th century by order of the Mercedarians under the pratonazgo of the Pizarro family and is a beautiful example of the Baroque in the town.

The Trujillo bullring is a 19th-century bullring located in the Spanish city of Trujillo, in the province of Cáceres. It is located on the outskirts of the town center on the route of the Cañada Real del Puerto de Miravete.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plaza_de_toros_de_Trujillo
Estanque de San Lázaro is a pond in Trujillo and has an altitude of 513 meters. It is located near Hostal Ruta De La Conquista, and near Trujillo.

The hermitage of San Lázaro is a hermitage from the 15th century.
The temple was built in 1498 outside the urban area to house a lazaretto. Over time, the building became an oratory dedicated to Cristo de la Salud, considered the patron saint of the city together with the Virgen de la Victoria.
https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ermita_de_San_L%C3%A1zaro_(Trujillo)
